pattern: Two priority queues simulate the queue: a free heap ordered by weight then index, and a busy heap ordered by free-time, advancing virtual time when no server is free.
hints: Process tasks in order; at the moment a task can start, give it to the free server with the smallest weight, breaking ties by smallest index.
Keep busy servers in a heap keyed by the time they become free so you can release them as time advances.
If no server is free when task j arrives, jump time forward to the next release; release every server free by the current time before assigning.

edge_inputs.description: tie on weight resolved by index
long task blocks then many short ones queue

stress.description: random servers and tasks of moderate length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    ns = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    nt = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    servers = [rng.randint(1, 200000) for _ in range(ns)]
    tasks = [rng.randint(1, 200) for _ in range(nt)]
    return [servers, tasks]

oracle_python:
import heapq
class Solution:
    def assignTasks(self, servers, tasks):
        free = [(w, i) for i, w in enumerate(servers)]
        heapq.heapify(free)
        busy = []  # (free_time, weight, index)
        m = len(tasks)
        ans = [0] * m
        t = 0
        for j in range(m):
            t = max(t, j)
            if not free:
                # advance to next release
                t = max(t, busy[0][0])
            while busy and busy[0][0] <= t:
                ft, w, idx = heapq.heappop(busy)
                heapq.heappush(free, (w, idx))
            w, idx = heapq.heappop(free)
            ans[j] = idx
            heapq.heappush(busy, (t + tasks[j], w, idx))
        return ans

solutions.python:
import heapq
class Solution:
    def assignTasks(self, servers, tasks):
        free = [(w, i) for i, w in enumerate(servers)]
        heapq.heapify(free)
        busy = []
        ans = []
        t = 0
        for j, dur in enumerate(tasks):
            t = max(t, j)
            while busy and busy[0][0] <= t:
                ft, w, idx = heapq.heappop(busy)
                heapq.heappush(free, (w, idx))
            if not free:
                t = busy[0][0]
                while busy and busy[0][0] <= t:
                    ft, w, idx = heapq.heappop(busy)
                    heapq.heappush(free, (w, idx))
            w, idx = heapq.heappop(free)
            ans.append(idx)
            heapq.heappush(busy, (t + dur, w, idx))
        return ans
